Title: Palm Tree Residences Miami
DescriptionLong: The first-ever branded residence from Palm Tree Crew, the global entertainment and hospitality company co-founded by world-renowned music producer Kygo and Myles Shear. Developed by PMG, Lion Development Group, Eden Residential, and Sterling Equities in Miami's entertainment district, the project will deliver 483 fully furnished and finished residences — from junior suites to three-bedroom floorplans — designed by Kobi Karp Architecture & Interior Design with custom Italian Italkraft cabinetry and a fully integrated Miele appliance package. The three-level amenity collection features a ground-floor Palm Tree Crew dining and nightlife venue, a rooftop infinity-edge pool overlooking Downtown Miami, an outdoor padel court, fitness center, climbing wall, cold plunges, saunas, and a yoga studio. Ownership unlocks priority access to Palm Tree Festivals worldwide, weekday dining privileges at Palm Tree Club Miami, and members-only access across the Palm Tree Crew hospitality portfolio. Sales launch the first week of June ahead of the FIFA World Cup in Miami.
Developer: Property Markets Group, Lion Development Group, Eden Residential, Sterling Equities

Title: Jean-Georges Miami Tropic Residences
Description: A 49-story, 338-unit luxury tower at the edge of the Miami Design District with culinary and lifestyle programming by two-Michelin-starred chef Jean-Georges Vongerichten.
DescriptionLong: Jean-Georges Miami Tropic Residences is a 49-story luxury condominium tower bringing 338 residences to the edge of Miami's Design District, within walkable reach of Wynwood, Midtown, and the neighborhood's anchor cultural institutions. Architecture by Arquitectonica with interiors by Yabu Pushelberg and landscape by CLAD shape a building immersed in tropical foliage, anchored by a private second-level porte-cochère and a ground floor housing Jean-Georges' inaugural abc kitchens in Miami. Two Michelin-starred chef Jean-Georges Vongerichten extends his culinary mastery across the building: residence kitchens are designed in his image, a residents-only indoor/outdoor restaurant anchors the pool deck on Level 8, and curated in-residence dining, catering menus, and pantry stocking carry the programming into private homes. Residences span Levels 9–47 with 10- to 11-foot floor-to-ceiling glass, private and semi-private elevator vestibules, and spacious balconies framing 180-degree views of Biscayne Bay, the Design District, and Downtown. The Penthouse Collection occupies Level 48, followed by a Rooftop Skydeck on Level 49 with pool, sky bar, archive library, and private dining room. Amenities also include a coworking floor with podcast studio and art gallery (Level 5), an indoor/outdoor fitness and spa floor with squash court and healing garden (Level 7), and resort-style pool deck with outdoor theater, golf simulator, and kids' programming (Level 8). Florida Green Building Coalition–aligned design. Developed by Terra and Lion, with exclusive sales by Douglas Elliman Development Marketing.

Title: 1250 West Avenue
Description: A 330-foot waterfront condominium tower on West Avenue in South Beach, replacing a 1964 building with 106 boutique-scale residences averaging nearly 3,500 square feet.
DescriptionLong: A 330-foot waterfront condominium tower planned for South Beach's West Avenue corridor was unanimously approved by the Miami Beach Design Review Board in May 2026. The development replaces an existing 15-story, 238-unit condominium completed in 1964 with a boutique 106-unit tower designed by Hollywood-based ODP Architecture and Design. Residences average nearly 3,500 square feet, with two penthouses crowned by private rooftop pools and amenity decks. The ground floor adds 2,685 square feet of commercial space, underground parking, and tropical gardens maintained by pollutant-filtered bioswales. As part of the previously approved overlay zoning agreement, the development team will complete its segment of the Baywalk and redevelop the adjacent Bikini Hostel site at 1247 West Avenue into a city park.
Architect: Odp Architecture and Design, ODP Architects
Developer: Terra, RG Development Group, GV Development

Title: Fulton Bond
Description: A full-block luxury condominium development in Fulton Market, designed by KPF with ParkFowler Plus and interiors by MAWD, planned across two phased towers rising to 33 stories.
DescriptionLong: Fulton Bond is a full-block condominium development at 1325 West Fulton Street in Chicago's Fulton Market District, bounded by North Ada Street to the west and North Elizabeth Street to the east. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox (KPF) in collaboration with ParkFowler Plus, the architecture interprets Fulton Market's industrial heritage through hand-set and precast brick arches, articulated podiums, and inverted bay windows expressed on the exterior as masonry arches — a contextual response rooted in the neighborhood's warehouse character. Interiors by MAWD / March and White Design layer warmth, curvature, and tonal richness across residences and shared spaces conceived to evoke a private home rather than a conventional condominium. Latest revisions (February 2026) reshape the project into two phased towers above a shared podium: a first-phase eastern tower of approximately 25 stories with a corrugated crown, followed by a 33-story western tower of approximately 500 feet clad in dark gray brick — together delivering roughly 149 to 240 residences across two- to four-bedroom layouts and penthouses, ranging from about 2,400 to over 4,500 square feet. Pricing guidance starts at $1.5M and runs into the mid-$7M range for penthouses. Shared amenities include a residents lounge with bar, fitness and wellness center, interactive golf simulator, outdoor pool with landscaped terrace, dog run, and 24-hour concierge. Developed by Sulo Development, which received City Council approval in early 2025 and completed an on-site sales center in 2025; presales are underway with delivery anticipated 2027–2029.
Architect: Kohn Pedersen Fox, Parkfowler Plus, Mawd

Title: DUS Padel
DescriptionLong:
A new 26,000-square-foot indoor padel club coming to 2031 Okeechobee Blvd in West Palm Beach, founded by Palm Beach County locals and designed as a hospitality-driven destination at the intersection of sport, wellness, and community. The club will feature five weather-protected indoor courts open to members and non-members, an organic café, men's and women's locker rooms with saunas and cold plunges, a spa-inspired recovery program led by licensed health professionals, and the first official NOX Pro Shop in the United States. Founding memberships are open now through Playtomic, starting at $225 per month or $2,250 per year for the first 100 members.</description_long>

Title: Soleste on the Trail

One- to three-bedroom apartments range from 700 to 1,000 square feet, finished with stainless steel appliances, quartz countertops, and walk-in closets. The community is built around a two-acre lake with a walking trail and a slate of resort-style amenities including a swimming pool, fitness center, spa, and children's playground — minutes from the shops and restaurants at The Square. Developed by 7920 Riviera Beach Holdings, an affiliate of Miami-based Estate Companies, which acquired the site for $8 million in 2022.

Title: The Setai, St. Maarten
Description: An ultra-luxury beachfront resort and branded residences in Indigo Bay, marking The Setai's Caribbean debut
DescriptionLong: The Setai St. Maarten is an ultra-luxury beachfront resort and collection of branded residences in Indigo Bay, developed by Altree Developments in partnership with The Setai Hotels (Nakash Family). Set along over 1,000 feet of pristine shoreline with views toward Saba and St. Barths, the property will feature a 205-key hotel plus 280 private residences ranging from approximately 620 to over 2,000 sq ft, with dual-key options up to 6,000 sq ft. Designed by HKS Architects with interiors by Studio Munge, amenities include a beach club with cabanas and butler service, three pools (including an adults-only oasis), a 30,000 sq ft destination spa, tennis and pickleball courts, five restaurant concepts, and a Les Clefs d'Or concierge team. It will be St. Maarten's first Leading Hotels of the World property. Construction is underway with completion expected in 2028.

Title: Arbor Coconut Grove
Description: A boutique 45-residence, 5-story building in the heart of Coconut Grove, steps from CocoWalk
DescriptionLong: Arbor Coconut Grove is a boutique 5-story, 45-residence building developed by Muse Groupe in the Village of Coconut Grove, Miami. Designed by Miami-based Behar Font & Partners, the low-density building offers spacious 2-, 3-, and 4-bedroom layouts with flexible den spaces, floor-to-ceiling impact-resistant windows, and nature-inspired modernist design. Located at 3034 Oak Avenue, the building is steps from CocoWalk, parks, marinas, top-rated schools, restaurants, and shops. Amenities include a pool, rooftop, lobby, pet-friendly community spaces, electric car charging stations, and ample resident parking. Move-in ready Q1 2026.

Title: DELUX WeHo
Description: A 134-unit boutique wellness-focused apartment community in Wedgewood-Houston with saltwater pool, red light saunas, cold plunge, and Technogym fitness center.
DescriptionLong: A 134-unit boutique apartment community in Nashville's Wedgewood-Houston neighborhood, developed by The Bradley Projects. Following the same wellness-driven approach as the team's earlier project The Lanes, DELUX WeHo prioritizes natural light, livability, and intentional living over scale. Residences range from approximately 500 to 1,500 square feet across studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom layouts, including loft-style floor plans that separate living and private spaces, with oversized windows and private balconies throughout. Wellness and recovery amenities include a saltwater pool, two red light saunas, a cold plunge, a dedicated recovery studio, and a Technogym fitness center. The property also features 5,000 square feet of street-level retail and sits within two blocks of Soho House Nashville, Michelin-starred Bastion, Hermès Nashville, and Pastis.

Title: Skyline OC
Description: Orange County's tallest residential building, a 25-story dual-tower condo conversion bringing resort-style ownership to South Coast Metro
DescriptionLong: Skyline OC is a 25-story, dual-tower condo conversion by Miami-based developer Crescent Heights, restoring ownership to Orange County's tallest residential building at 9 MacArthur Place in Santa Ana. Originally built in 2010 as condominiums and later operated as rentals, the towers have been fully reimagined with sophisticated interiors, hotel-caliber amenities, and an ownership-focused resident culture. Move-in-ready residences range from one- and two-bedroom homes to penthouses up to $3 million, with prices starting around $600,000. Average unit size is approximately 1,500 square feet — significantly larger than newly built condos in comparable markets — and homes feature floor-to-ceiling windows, open layouts, and chef-worthy kitchens with Viking, LG, and Miele appliances. The 60,000 square feet of amenity space includes an 80-foot pool with teak lounges and private cabanas, a fitness center with yoga and Pilates studios, spa treatment rooms, sauna and steam areas, a bocce court, putting green, co-working lounge, and chef's kitchen. Services include 24-hour security and an on-site concierge. Sales are led by Polaris Pacific. Located near John Wayne Airport, UC Irvine, and Newport Beach.

Title: DUOS Wynwood
Description: A new 49-unit, eight-story short-term rental hospitality concept rising in the heart of Miami's Wynwood Arts District
DescriptionLong: A new 49-unit, eight-story short-term rental hospitality concept in the heart of Miami's Wynwood Arts District. DUOS was purpose-designed from the ground up specifically for short-term rentals—allowing buyers to fully own their unit while renting it out for stays ranging from a single night up to 30 days. Architecture by MKDA and interiors by award-winning international hospitality design firm AvroKo create a non-branded, design-driven identity rooted in eclectic modernism. The 49-unit scale offers a curated, boutique alternative to Miami's larger 200-plus-unit developments, with studio to two-bedroom suites ranging from the $500,000s to over $1 million. Construction is expected to break ground in late 2026, with completion anticipated in early 2028.
Developer: Wave Group Development, Vitrium Capital, Zambala Luxury Apartments

DescriptionLong: Toronto's first supertall, an 85-story, 1,013-ft Foster + Partners tower at Yonge and Bloor with a Lanvin retail base and 416 condos, and the brand-new Andaz Toronto.

Title: Burj Azizi
Description: A 2,379-ft, 133-story Sheikh Zayed Road megatall poised to be the world's second-tallest, holding a seven-star themed hotel, branded residences and a vertical mall, completing 2028.
DescriptionLong: A 2,379-ft, 133-story Sheikh Zayed Road megatall poised to be the world's second-tallest, holding a seven-star themed hotel, branded residences and a vertical mall, completing 2028.

Title: The Standard, Lisbon
Description: A 173-room Standard hotel reimagined inside the 16th-century Palácio Santa Clara above the Royal Navy Hospital ruins in Alfama, with Jaime Hayon interiors and a Tagus-view rooftop, opening 2026.
DescriptionLong: A 173-room Standard hotel reimagined inside the 16th-century Palácio Santa Clara above the Royal Navy Hospital ruins in Alfama, with Jaime Hayon interiors and a Tagus-view rooftop, opening 2026.
Architect: Samuel Torres de Carvalho; Jaime Hayon (interiors)

Title: New AS Roma Stadium
Description: AS Roma's 60,605-seat Populous-designed stadium in Pietralata, inspired by classical Roman architecture and home to Europe's largest single stand, with first match targeted for 2031.
DescriptionLong: AS Roma's 60,605-seat Populous-designed stadium in Pietralata, inspired by classical Roman architecture and home to Europe's largest single stand, with first match targeted for 2031.

Title: Guggenheim Abu Dhabi
Description: Frank Gehry's largest Guggenheim — a 320,000-sq-ft cluster of nine sculptural metallic cones rising on Saadiyat Island for a 2026 opening.
DescriptionLong: Frank Gehry's largest Guggenheim — a 320,000-sq-ft cluster of nine sculptural metallic cones rising on Saadiyat Island for a 2026 opening.
Developer: Department of Culture and Tourism – Abu Dhabi

Title: Zayed National Museum
Description: Foster + Partners' falcon-winged museum on Saadiyat Cultural District, opened December 2025, telling the UAE's national story across five soaring solar towers.
DescriptionLong: Foster + Partners' falcon-winged museum on Saadiyat Cultural District, opened December 2025, telling the UAE's national story across five soaring solar towers.
Developer: Department of Culture and Tourism – Abu Dhabi

Title: New San Siro Stadium
Description: Foster + Partners and MANICA's 71,500-seat home for AC Milan and Inter Milan — twin great rings replacing the legendary Giuseppe Meazza, targeted to debut for Euro 2032.
DescriptionLong: Foster + Partners and MANICA's 71,500-seat home for AC Milan and Inter Milan — twin great rings replacing the legendary Giuseppe Meazza, targeted to debut for Euro 2032.

Title: Sindalah
Description: NEOM's first completed showcase — a Luca Dini–designed Red Sea luxury island with 440 rooms, 88 villas, a yacht club by Stefano Ricci, and golf, beach, and beach-club destinations.
DescriptionLong: NEOM's first completed showcase — a Luca Dini–designed Red Sea luxury island with 440 rooms, 88 villas, a yacht club by Stefano Ricci, and golf, beach, and beach-club destinations.

Title: Wasl Tower
Description: UNStudio's 302-meter contrapposto-twisted Sheikh Zayed Road landmark wrapped in a ceramic-fin facade, completed alongside Burj Khalifa as Dubai's newest sculptural skyscraper.
DescriptionLong: UNStudio's 302-meter contrapposto-twisted Sheikh Zayed Road landmark wrapped in a ceramic-fin facade, completed alongside Burj Khalifa as Dubai's newest sculptural skyscraper.

Title: Ciel Dubai Marina
Description: NORR's 377-meter Dubai Marina supertall — Guinness-recognized as the world's tallest hotel, a 1,042-room five-star with a vertical-garden atrium and 360-degree sky observation deck.
DescriptionLong: NORR's 377-meter Dubai Marina supertall — Guinness-recognized as the world's tallest hotel, a 1,042-room five-star with a vertical-garden atrium and 360-degree sky observation deck.

Title: Brisbane Olympic Stadium
Description: Cox Architecture, Hassell, and Azusa Sekkei's 63,000-seat Victoria Park venue — a $3.8 billion stadium-in-the-landscape inspired by the Queensland verandah and the centerpiece of the 2032 Games.
DescriptionLong: Cox Architecture, Hassell, and Azusa Sekkei's 63,000-seat Victoria Park venue — a $3.8 billion stadium-in-the-landscape inspired by the Queensland verandah and the centerpiece of the 2032 Games.
Developer: Queensland Government, Games Independent Infrastructure and Coordination Authority

Title: Marina Bay Sands Expansion
Description: Safdie Architects' 55-story fourth tower at the iconic Marina Bay Sands — 570 ultra-luxury suites with triple-height sky terraces, paired with a Populous-designed 15,000-seat arena.
DescriptionLong: Safdie Architects' 55-story fourth tower at the iconic Marina Bay Sands — 570 ultra-luxury suites with triple-height sky terraces, paired with a Populous-designed 15,000-seat arena.
Developer: Marina Bay Sands (Las Vegas Sands Corp.)

Title: Cabot Revelstoke
Description: Cabot's first four-season mountain golf-and-ski resort — a 155-key alpine lodge, an 18-hole Rod Whitman course, a par-3 short course, and luxury chalet residences set among British Columbia's Monashees and Selkirks.
DescriptionLong: Anchored in the alpine town of Revelstoke — North America's largest vertical drop and self-styled 'heli-skiing capital of the world' — Cabot's first four-season mountain destination weaves a 155-room Mountain Lodge, an 18-hole championship course, a par-3 short course, and an exclusive collection of alpine chalets across a dramatic bench above the Columbia River valley. The Mountain Lodge (early 2027) features a modern steakhouse and bar, full spa and wellness with hot tubs and sauna, a fitness center, a European-inspired event venue for up to 400 guests, curated retail, and combined check-in for golf and heli-skiing. The 18-hole Cabot Revelstoke course (preview play fall 2026) winds along cliffs, creeks, and natural landforms with generous fairways and bold bunkering, certified through Audubon's Platinum Signature Sanctuary program. The Railyard, a par-3 short course paying homage to Revelstoke's railway heritage, anchors a vibrant clubhouse with dining, bowling, virtual golf, and shuffleboard. The Residences — Dolomites/Alps-inspired chalets with soaring ceilings, expansive terraces, and panoramic mountain views — debut with Chalet 1's nine four- and five-bedroom single-level homes (four-bedrooms from $7.6M CAD).

Title: Eastpoint
Description: Parcel C of Eastpoint — gateway phase of Nashville's 30-acre East Bank neighborhood next to the new Nissan Stadium, anchored by a 1.5-acre public plaza with 50,000 sq ft of retail, a 600-room hotel, and residential.
DescriptionLong: Parcel C of Eastpoint is the gateway phase of a 30-acre East Bank neighborhood rising directly across the Cumberland River from downtown Nashville, adjacent to the new Nissan Stadium and the future Tennessee Performing Arts Center. The plan centers on a 1.5-acre public plaza framed by approximately 50,000 square feet of multi-level retail and restaurant space, tree-lined sidewalks, dedicated bike lanes, and below-ground parking that lifts pedestrian life to the street level. Above it, the parcel will deliver a 600-room full-service hotel alongside a residential component with 10% set aside as affordable housing — designed to serve full-time residents while absorbing the crowds drawn to the stadium and TPAC across the year.
ProjectType: Residences, Hotel, Entertainment, Eateries, Park, Office
Architect: HKS, Michael Hsu Office of Architecture, Gresham Smith, Morris Adjmi Architects

Title: 520 Fifth Avenue
Description: KPF's 1,002-foot Midtown Manhattan supertall — a slender, stone-and-glass spiraling tower with arched cascading windows topped by a copper-finned crown, holding 100 condominiums above 25 boutique office floors and the members-only Moss Club.
DescriptionLong: On a tight Fifth Avenue parcel between 43rd and 44th Streets, Rabina's 520 Fifth Avenue rises 88 storeys / 1,002 feet (305 m) — the seventh-tallest Manhattan tower at completion. KPF's design wraps the 415,000-sq-ft tower in a spiraling masonry envelope of arched windows that step in cascading setbacks toward a ribbed copper crown. The program stacks 25 boutique-office floors and ground-floor retail beneath 100 condominium residences and amenities by Charles & Co. and AvroKO including the new Moss Club — a private members lounge spanning the two cellar levels and the first three floors above grade. Façade nearing completion late 2025; closings tracking 2026.

Title: The Brooklyn Tower
Description: SHoP Architects' bronze-and-black 1,066-foot Brooklyn supertall at 9 DeKalb — a cinematic interlocking-hexagon massing rising over the landmarked 1908 Dime Savings Bank, holding 398 condos and 150 rentals.
DescriptionLong: Brooklyn's first supertall climbs 1,066 feet / 93 stories above the landmarked Beaux-Arts Dime Savings Bank of Brooklyn (1908) at the convergence of DeKalb, Flatbush and Fleet, where Downtown Brooklyn meets Fort Greene. SHoP Architects designed an extruded interlocking-hexagon massing clad in shimmering bronze, blackened steel and copper — its convex/concave crown reading as a Gotham-City silhouette across the East River. JDS Development Group developed the 800,000-sq-ft tower; Silverstein Properties took control of the unsold inventory in June 2024. The mixed-tenure program holds 398 condominiums (interiors by Gachot Studios) above 150 rentals, with amenities by Woods Bagot, including a 27,000-sq-ft 'Society' wellness floor inside the historic banking hall. Topped out 2021, occupancy from late 2022.

Title: Six Senses Napa Valley
Description: Olson Kundig's heritage-led 750-acre Aetna Springs revival in northern Napa County — Six Senses' first West Coast wine-country resort with 95 keys, 16 branded residences, restored 1890s mineral springs and 1,000 acres of rewilded oak woodland.
DescriptionLong: Set deep in Pope Valley north of Calistoga, Six Senses Napa Valley reanimates the 1873 Aetna Springs resort — the oldest continuously operating resort in California and a Napa County Threatened Treasure — across a 3,000-acre estate. Olson Kundig leads the architectural restoration of nine historic buildings, with new pavilions, ten tented suites, 16 branded residences (interiors by 1508 London) and a wellness village around restored thermal mineral springs that include indoor and outdoor bathing, mud pools and herb-and-essential-oil therapies. Dining includes a communal-table Dining Hall and The Well — an open-air space celebrating the property's history with mineral-water bottling and distilling. Developer team: Weller Development Partners and Mohari Hospitality, operated by Six Senses (IHG Hotels & Resorts). Targeting 2026 opening.

Title: Naples Beach Club, A Four Seasons Resort
Description: Hart Howerton's 125-acre Gulf Coast revival of the historic Naples Beach Hotel — Four Seasons' only Florida Gulf Coast resort with 220 keys, 153 branded residences, a Tom Fazio-designed 18-hole course and 1,000 feet of private beach.
DescriptionLong: On 125 oceanfront acres on Gulf Shore Boulevard in Naples, the Athens Group's redevelopment of the legendary 1946 Naples Beach Hotel & Golf Club delivers Four Seasons' only Gulf Coast resort. Master-planned and designed by Hart Howerton with interiors by Champalimaud, the property reinterprets Naples' historic cottage vernacular at hotel scale — shellcrete walls, cypress and pecky-cypress millwork, white-glazed terra cotta and a hand-painted royal palm mural in the lobby. The program includes 220 hotel rooms and suites with 1,000 feet of private beach, 153 luxury residences across three new beachside buildings and five golfside buildings, eight restaurants and bars, two pools, a 27,000-sq-ft Spa & Wellness center, family programming and a re-designed 100-acre Tom Fazio championship golf course (The Gardens) opening 2026. Hotel debuted late 2025; further openings throughout 2026.

Title: Rosewood Half Moon Bay Antigua
Description: Studio Piet Boon's 132-acre ultra-luxury Caribbean enclave on Half Moon Bay's coral-sand crescent — 47 pavilion suites and a collection of two-to-five-bedroom Rosewood Residences set in landscapes by VITA across one of Antigua's most protected coves.
DescriptionLong: Sited on a private 132-acre headland enclosing Antigua's mile-long Half Moon Bay — a UNESCO-coved coral beach on the island's southeast coast — the Rosewood Half Moon Bay reimagines a long-dormant 1960s resort as a flagship Caribbean Rosewood. Studio Piet Boon designs the architecture and interiors of both the 47 pavilion-style hotel suites and the Rosewood Residences, ranging from two- to five-bedroom homes and four-bedroom beachfront villas with direct sand access; landscape architecture by VITA Inc. weaves restored coastal forest, lagoons and amenities including a beach club, kids club, spa village, signature restaurants and a marina-front yacht club. Developed by Replay Destinations with Replay Resorts; opening targeted, with Residences sales actively underway.

Title: Lucas Museum of Narrative Art
Description: MAD Architects' biomorphic 300,000-sq-ft 'spaceship' museum in LA's Exposition Park — George Lucas's $1B+ permanent home for narrative art with 1,500 hand-finished FRP cloud panels and a public canopy hovering above 11 acres of restored parkland.
DescriptionLong: Conceived by Ma Yansong of MAD Architects as an extension of Exposition Park's tree canopy, the five-story, 27,900-square-meter Lucas Museum of Narrative Art opens September 22, 2026 as the centerpiece of Los Angeles's Exposition Park, a few blocks from USC and the LA Memorial Coliseum. The undulating exterior is wrapped in more than 1,500 curved fiberglass-reinforced polymer (FRP) panels — each robotically fabricated and hand-finished — that lift at the building's center to form a sheltered public plaza. Inside are 9,290 sq m of galleries, two state-of-the-art theaters, ten education studios, library, restaurant, museum shop and a rooftop event space beneath a ribbed 'cloud' ceiling. The permanent collection holds more than 40,000 works — Norman Rockwell, Frida Kahlo, Jack Kirby, Gordon Parks, Dorothea Lange — alongside cinematic artifacts. Landscape architecture by Studio-MLA's Mia Lehrer; Stantec serves as executive architect; principal benefactors George Lucas and Mellody Hobson.
Developer: Lucas Museum of Narrative Art (George Lucas, Mellody Hobson)

Title: Hudson's Detroit
Description: SHoP Architects' transformation of Detroit's long-vacant J.L. Hudson's Department Store site — a 1.5M-sq-ft mixed-use complex with a 49-story, 681-foot Hudson's Tower (Michigan's tallest) holding an EDITION hotel and condos above a 12-story office and event block anchored by GM's new global HQ.
DescriptionLong: Hudson's Detroit reimagines the long-vacant block of the demolished J.L. Hudson's Department Store at 1208 Woodward Avenue downtown — once the world's tallest department store. Bedrock-developed, SHoP Architects-designed, the 1.5-million-sq-ft complex pairs the recently completed 12-story, 232-foot Block Building (1240 Woodward) — anchored by General Motors's new global HQ on floors 8-11, with a 56,000-sq-ft event venue 'The Department at Hudson's' and a seven-story atrium capped by a Corvette-headlight skylight — with the 49-story, 681-foot Hudson's Tower, the tallest building in Michigan, holding an EDITION hotel (~227 rooms) and 97 private condominiums above retail. Materials reference Detroit's early-20th-century skyline: glass, steel and white-glazed terracotta echoing the Guardian and Penobscot Buildings. First phase completed October 2025; tower opening targeted 2027. New 'Nick Gilbert Way' pedestrian plaza opens November 2025.

Title: Nu Stadium at Miami Freedom Park
Description: MANICA + Arquitectonica's $350M soccer-specific home for David Beckham's Inter Miami CF — a 26,700-seat single-tier bowl crowned by a single white membrane roof reading as Major League Soccer's largest canopy, opened April 4, 2026 as centerpiece of the 131-acre Freedom Park district.
DescriptionLong: Set on the site of the former Melreese Country Club golf course adjacent to Miami International Airport, Nu Stadium at Miami Freedom Park serves as Inter Miami CF's permanent home — the club David Beckham co-founded and Lionel Messi made global. MANICA leads design with Arquitectonica as architect of record (and master planner of the 131-acre Freedom Park district); Thornton Tomasetti structural. The 26,700-seat bowl is single-tiered with a glazed-edge white membrane roof that extends past the seating to cover an elevated concourse — the largest canopy in MLS. Pink-accented seating, a glass players' tunnel viewable from the premium Nu Club, retractable lower north stand, two large curved video boards above the goals, and a Leo Messi Stand on the east side. Brazilian digital bank Nubank acquired naming rights. Cost ~$350M; construction 2023-2026; first MLS match April 4, 2026 vs. Austin FC. Future Freedom Park phases add a 58-acre public park, hotels, retail and entertainment.
Developer: Miami Freedom Park LLC (David Beckham, Jorge & Jose Mas)

Title: New Kansas City Royals Stadium
Description: Populous's $1.9B fountain-and-Crown-Vision downtown ballpark for the Kansas City Royals, breaking ground 2027 on Hallmark's Crown Center campus as the 85-acre, $3B+ centerpiece of a co-developed mixed-use district anchoring both Royals and Hallmark headquarters.
DescriptionLong: On April 22, 2026, the Kansas City Royals and Hallmark Cards jointly announced the new Crown Center ballpark district — an 85-acre, $3B+ mixed-use development to rise on the site of Hallmark's current corporate headquarters. The conceptual renderings (designed by hometown architecture firm Populous) feature a downtown ballpark with a central fountain plaza, a Crown Vision scoreboard and seamless integration with reimagined Hallmark and Royals headquarters. The $1.9B stadium itself is targeted for opening day 2030, with construction beginning in early 2027; the broader district will deliver more than 20,000 construction jobs and over $2B in private investment. Notably, the 85-acre site preserves the historic Our Lady of Sorrows Parish church directly adjacent to the new ballpark.

Title: Burj Jumeira
Description: SOM's 550-meter (1,804-foot) split-volume Dubai supertall designed for state-owned Dubai Holding — a curvilinear two-mass tower rising from a reflecting pool emblazoned with Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum's fingerprint, with a digital-display façade, sky restaurants and a pearl-evoking observation deck atop Downtown Jumeira.
DescriptionLong: Conceived as the centerpiece of the new SOM-masterplanned Downtown Jumeira mixed-use district in Al Sufouh, Burj Jumeira (also Burj Jumeirah) is a 550-meter / 1,804-foot supertall by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill's New York office. The aerodynamic split-volume tower features a large void between two curvilinear masses — a form referencing regional dunes and oases — rising from an oval-shaped pond emblazoned with the fingerprint of Dubai's ruler, Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, encircled by water-feature shops and a terraced outdoor amphitheatre. The façade is to carry an integrated digital display, with a 450-meter sky lounge and sky restaurant and a spherical observation deck evocative of the native Gulf pearl. State-owned Dubai Holding announced the project in January 2019; SOM provides architectural and structural services as well as the surrounding Downtown Jumeira master plan.

Title: Universal Epic Universe
Description: Universal's $7B fourth Orlando theme park — the first major new U.S. park in 25 years — opened May 22, 2025 with a hub-and-spoke layout of five worlds (Celestial Park, Super Nintendo World, Ministry of Magic, Dark Universe, How to Train Your Dragon — Isle of Berk) anchored by the Chronos icon tower.
DescriptionLong: Officially opened May 22, 2025, Universal Epic Universe is Universal Destinations and Experiences' fourth Orlando theme park and the most ambitious park the operator has ever built — the first all-new major U.S. theme park in 25 years, with cost estimates around $7B. The park employs a hub-and-spoke layout: at the center sits Celestial Park, a fountain-and-pond garden themed to interdimensional travel and crowned by the Chronos tower, the park's gateway icon. From the hub, four portals lead to themed lands — Super Nintendo World; The Wizarding World of Harry Potter — Ministry of Magic (a 1920s Parisian streetscape with the British Ministry of Magic); Dark Universe (Universal Classic Monsters); and How to Train Your Dragon — Isle of Berk. Approximately 50 attractions, entertainment, dining and retail experiences across the campus, plus the on-site Helios Grand Hotel. Headline rides include Stardust Racers, a dual-launch racing coaster reaching 62 mph and 133 ft. Universal won four 2025 Thea Awards for the park.

Title: Suzhou Museum of Contemporary Art
Description: BIG's 60,000-sq-m 'Chinese knot' of 12 pavilions and courtyards woven beneath an undulating ribbon-like roof of stainless tiles along Suzhou's Jinji Lake — a fifth-facade museum opening 2026 with the BIG-curated 'Materialism' inaugural exhibition.
DescriptionLong: Set along the Jinji Lake waterfront in Suzhou's Industrial Park district, the 60,000-square-meter Suzhou Museum of Contemporary Art (Suzhou MoCA) by BIG — Bjarke Ingels Group is conceived as a garden of 12 pavilions and courtyards woven together by glazed galleries and porticoes — what Ingels describes as a 'Chinese knot' of interconnected sculpture courts and exhibition spaces. The roof's gentle conical undulations recall traditional tiled eaves, and from above (particularly from the nearby Suzhou Ferris wheel) the warm-toned stainless tiles read as a distinctive fifth facade branching out like a rhizome to connect city and lake. Façades combine rippled curved glass with stainless steel. The museum will debut with 'Materialism,' a BIG-curated inaugural exhibition tracing a material journey from stone to recyclate, ahead of the official 2026 public opening.

Title: Studio Museum
Description: Adjaye Associates + Cooper Robertson's $160M, 82,000-sq-ft, seven-story purpose-built home for The Studio Museum on West 125th Street — a brownstone-referencing facade with a stepped 'reverse stoop,' a Studio Zewde-designed rooftop terrace, and more than doubled gallery and Artist-in-Residence space.
DescriptionLong: Opened to the public on November 15, 2025, the new Studio Museum in Harlem is the first purpose-built home for the celebrated 1968-founded museum of African-American art, on West 125th Street between Lenox and Adam Clayton Powell, Jr. Boulevards. Designed by Adjaye Associates in collaboration with Cooper Robertson, the seven-story, 82,000-square-foot, $160-million building reinterprets the masonry patterns and window compositions of Harlem's brownstones and prewar apartment buildings; a series of glass doors and stepped entryways recall the social stoops that define the area's streetscape, with a generous double-height ground-floor window and a 'reverse stoop' descending into the museum to dissolve the boundary between sidewalk and lobby. Exhibition and Artist-in-Residence spaces have more than doubled, allowing larger-scale work; a rooftop terrace by Harlem-based Studio Zewde adds a landscaped gathering space with native plantings and panoramic Manhattan views.

Title: David Geffen Galleries at LACMA
Description: Peter Zumthor's $724M horizontal, free-flowing concrete-and-glass single-floor museum spanning Wilshire Boulevard at LACMA — a 220,000-sq-ft non-hierarchical gallery elevated 30 feet above street level on seven semi-transparent pavilions, opening April 19, 2026 to house the museum's permanent collection.
DescriptionLong: Designed by Pritzker Prize laureate Peter Zumthor in collaboration with Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, the David Geffen Galleries are a $724M concrete-and-glass single-floor building that curves along Hancock Park and spans Wilshire Boulevard on LACMA's 20-acre Miracle Mile campus — the culmination of a more than two-decade transformation. The free-flowing main floor is elevated nearly 30 feet above street level on seven semi-transparent pavilions that hold a theater, retail, dining, education facilities and outdoor programming around the W.M. Keck Foundation Plaza. The horizontal, single-level layout deliberately collapses traditional museum hierarchies, placing all works on the same plane with naturally lit perimeter galleries and fully enclosed interior rooms. Major construction wrapped at the end of 2024; portions of the lower levels began opening to the public in summer 2025; LACMA expands its gallery space from ~130,000 to 220,000 sq ft. Targets LEED Gold; over 95% construction-and-demolition waste diverted; grand public opening April 19, 2026.
Architect: Peter Zumthor with Skidmore, Owings & Merrill
Developer: Los Angeles County Museum of Art (LACMA)

Title: Kawonu Golf Club
Description: Andrew Green's first entirely original 18-hole course on a 294-acre Reedy River site in southern Greenville County — a private, golf-only club for ~500 members with a Joel Newman / Thomas and Denzinger clubhouse and on-site cottages, breaking ground 2025 for a 2027 opening.
DescriptionLong: Set on 294 rolling acres west of Fountain Inn at the intersection of S.C. Highway 418 and Fork Shoals Road, Kawonu Golf Club is the first new private golf club to be built in Greenville in roughly 30 years. Renowned course architect Andrew Green — best known for the championship-grade renovations of Congressional Country Club's Blue Course, East Lake Golf Club, Oak Hill, Inverness and Scioto — leads design of his first entirely original 18-hole course on a stunning piece of ground featuring rolling terrain, boulder-topped bluffs, woodlands and ponds bordering the Reedy River, with about 100 acres preserved as nature preserve and irrigation drawn largely from on-site ponds and harvested rainwater. Joel Newman of Thomas and Denzinger Architects designs the clubhouse, cottages and supporting facilities (Newman's portfolio includes Chechessee Creek Club and Ohoopee Match Club). Founded by Scott Ferrell (Greenville resident, 35-year golf veteran and former president of Gary Player Design) and real-estate developer Barton Tuck, the club is structured for ~500 memberships (350 local, 150 national) plus on-site cottage stays. Construction began early 2025; opening targeted for 2027.

Title: Navy SEAL Museum San Diego
Description: An 85,000-square-foot, four-level waterfront museum proposed for San Diego's North Embarcadero, with an angular, ship-shaped form and perforated metal facade designed by ZGF Architects.
DescriptionLong: The Navy SEAL Museum San Diego is a four-level, 85,000-square-foot waterfront museum proposed for 1220 Pacific Highway along the city's North Embarcadero, alongside a new 150-foot public park extending Lane Field Park toward the harbor. The angular, ship-shaped structure features metallic volumes, ribboned facade window reveals, perforated metal screens that filter natural light into the interior, and a 65-foot-tall central atrium, with immersive technology-forward exhibits flowing from upper-level galleries down to a fourth-floor rooftop terrace with sweeping harbor views. The $256 million project is privately financed by the museum's nonprofit parent, with construction targeted to begin in early 2029 and completion projected for 2031–2032.
Developer: Navy SEAL Museum San Diego, Hensel Phelps

Title: 2 World Trade Center
Description: Foster + Partners' newly redesigned 1,224-foot supertall at the World Trade Center campus — a stepped, terraced glass tower set to become American Express's new global HQ, breaking ground spring 2026.
DescriptionLong: Rising 373 meters at 200 Greenwich Street in Lower Manhattan, Two World Trade Center is the final commercial tower of the World Trade Center master plan and the future global headquarters of American Express. Foster + Partners' updated design — released in February 2026 — presents a stepped composition of slender volumes rising from a rectilinear base, articulated by a sequence of landscaped terraces and green roofs that distribute more than an acre of outdoor space throughout the building. The 55-story, ~186,000-square-meter tower will accommodate up to 10,000 employees and is positioned directly across from Calatrava's Oculus and the Perelman Performing Arts Center. Planned as a fully electric building with advanced smart-building systems pursuing LEED certification, the project draws on BIG's stacked-block concept and Foster's earlier crown studies, merging into a hybrid composition. Construction is scheduled to begin in spring 2026, with completion targeted for 2031.

Title: Atlantis The Royal
Description: KPF's quarter-mile-long Palm Jumeirah resort and residences — a 43-story stacked-block lattice with cantilevered sky pools, 795 guestrooms, 231 branded residences and Dubai's most extravagant launch event.
DescriptionLong: Set on Dubai's Palm Jumeirah, Atlantis The Royal is a 193,600-square-meter hospitality icon that reinvented the language of the tall building. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox's New York office (lead architects James von Klemperer and Elie Gamburg), the 43-story project organizes a series of shifted, stacked residential and hotel blocks into a sinuous quarter-mile lattice, with the gaps between volumes forming sky terraces, cabanas and the now-iconic cantilevered infinity pools — including an 88th-meter Cleopatra-style sky pool. The resort houses 795 guestrooms and suites alongside 231 branded Atlantis residences, 17 restaurants and bars (including outposts by José Andrés, Heston Blumenthal, Costas Spiliadis, Ariana Bundy and more), a vast spa, 90 swimming experiences and one of the largest fountain-and-pyrotechnic shows in the region by WET Design. Developed by Kerzner International with Besix–Ssangyong JV as main contractor and interiors by GA Design and SM Design, Atlantis The Royal opened in 2023 with a star-studded debut headlined by Beyoncé.

Title: Six Senses London at The Whiteley
Description: Six Senses' flagship UK debut inside Foster + Partners' £1bn restoration of The Whiteley in Bayswater — a 109-room hotel and 14 residences with EPR/AvroKO interiors above 139 luxury homes.
DescriptionLong: Set within The Whiteley — the Foster + Partners-led restoration of the 1911 grade II*-listed Whiteley's department store on Queensway in Bayswater — Six Senses London is the brand's first UK property and its global wellness flagship. EPR Architects serves as hotel architect with AvroKO leading interior design, contributing a restrained palette of warm woods, parquet flooring and Art Deco references that wrap 109 rooms and suites, 14 one- and two-bedroom branded residences, and the brand's first urban Six Senses Place social-wellness club. Amenities include a 20-meter pool, 24-hour gym, screening room, co-working lounges, a beauty studio, multiple dining rooms and a spa featuring sound healing, biohacking and longevity programming. The building's reborn marble grand staircase — deconstructed, lengthened and rebuilt by hand in Devon by Foster + Partners — ascends three floors to a restored glass-domed atrium. Six Senses London is part of the wider 1-million-square-foot Whiteley mixed-use district led by Valouran with MARK and C C Land, which also includes 139 Foster + Partners-designed residences, 60,000 sq ft of amenities and a curated collection of 19 commercial brands.

Title: One Bangkok
Description: SOM's $3.9bn, 41-acre 'city within a city' beside Lumphini Park — Thailand's largest private development, with five offices, three hotels, retail podiums and a future 437-meter Signature Tower.
DescriptionLong: One Bangkok is Thailand's largest private-sector development — a $3.9 billion, 16.7-hectare (41-acre) mixed-use district master-planned and master-architected by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) at the intersection of Wireless Road, Rama IV and Sathorn, steps from Lumphini Park. Developed by TCC Assets and Frasers Property and built in collaboration with Architects 49 (A49), KPF, Chapman Taylor and Lead8, the first phase combines eight skyscrapers and a series of smaller pavilions around a central civic plaza, delivering five office towers, three hotels/serviced-apartment towers, an art-and-culture quarter, interconnected retail podiums, and 8 hectares of plazas and landscaped greenspace including the preserved Wireless House. Phase two will add SOM's 92-storey, 437-meter (1,430-ft) Signature Tower — set to become the tallest building in Thailand on completion. Climate-responsive design, a central utility plant, sponge parks engineered for a 1-in-500-year flood event, and Thailand's first LEED Platinum Neighborhood Development certification anchor the development, while a permanent public art program features Anish Kapoor, Sir Tony Cragg and Alex Face among others. Phased opening began October 2024 with full delivery through 2030.

Title: Lusail Plaza Towers
Description: Foster + Partners' four aluminum-clad supertalls anchoring Lusail's new central business district — twisting elliptical forms targeting 4-star GSAS, with the tallest 301m and the tallest in Qatar.
DescriptionLong: Conceived as the catalyst for Lusail's new central business district — 10 miles north of Doha and at the end of the grand boulevard linking Lusail Stadium to the corniche — Lusail Plaza Towers is Foster + Partners' 1.1-million-square-meter quartet of high-rises arranged symmetrically around a central plaza. Two 70-story towers (the tallest at 301 m, making them Qatar's tallest buildings) flank two 50-story towers and a series of podium buildings filled with shops, cafés and restaurants. The elliptical footprints morph through 90 degrees as they rise, with a central concrete core and sixteen inclined perimeter columns generating the geometry; marine-grade aluminum cladding and twisting external shading fins reduce solar exposure by ~35%, while phase-change-material thermal storage, demand-controlled ventilation and high-pressure hydronic systems target a 4-star regional Global Sustainability Assessment System (GSAS) rating. The towers will house the headquarters of Qatar National Bank, Qatar Central Bank, the Qatar Investment Authority and Qatari Diar, alongside other global organizations, anchoring a new financial and civic core for the city.

Title: Shanghai Grand Opera House
Description: Snøhetta's helical-roofed riverside opera house with three auditoriums — opening late 2026 on the Huangpu River, with a spiraling rooftop pathway and 2,000-seat main hall by Nagata Acoustics.
DescriptionLong: Now nearing completion on the convex bank of the Huangpu River in Shanghai's Expo Houtan district, the Shanghai Grand Opera House is Snøhetta's competition-winning civic landmark — first awarded in 2017 and developed in consortium with East China Architectural Design & Research Institute (ECADI), Theatre Projects and Nagata Acoustics. A sweeping helical roof wraps the site in a single continuous gesture, extending beyond the building's enclosed volumes to form an elevated, publicly accessible civic platform; a spiraling pathway leads visitors from ground level to an observation deck overlooking the Huangpu and the Shanghai skyline. Beneath the roof, three auditoriums anchor the program: a 2,000-seat main hall designed for opera and large-scale productions and tuned to international acoustic standards by Nagata, a 1,200-seat secondary hall and a 1,000-seat flexible black-box theater. Public foyers feature expansive glazed facades, oak flooring and dark-stained wood that supports acoustic performance, alongside restaurants, exhibition spaces, education facilities and screening rooms. Opening is anticipated in the second half of 2026, with the project among ArchDaily's most-anticipated completions of the year.

Title: The Whale
Description: Dorte Mandrup's Arctic cultural center on Andøya in northern Norway — a parabolic stone-and-vegetation roof that emerges from the coastal landscape as a whale-watching attraction.
DescriptionLong: Located approximately 300 kilometers north of the Arctic Circle on the island of Andøya near the village of Andenes — within sight of the Bleiksdjupa deep-sea canyon that draws sperm whales close to shore — The Whale is a cultural and scientific center designed by Danish practice Dorte Mandrup with Marianne Levinsen Landscape, Ralph Appelbaum Associates (exhibits), Norconsult and AT Plan & Arkitektur. The building emerges from the rocky shoreline as a single sculpted gesture: a parabolic roof formed by a series of curved steel-and-concrete ribs that span a column-free interior with limited points of support, engineered for the site's wind and snow loads and finished with locally sourced stone and vegetation so the structure visually extends the terrain. The accessible roof functions as a publicly walkable viewing platform sloping toward the Norwegian Sea and surrounding archipelago. Inside, exhibition galleries — devoted to whales and marine ecosystems — open through large glazed walls toward the water, alongside offices, a café, and a retail area, with outdoor paths and viewpoints extending into the landscape. The visitor attraction is currently under construction with an anticipated opening in 2027.

Title: Village Landing
Description: A massive 71-acre mixed-use walkable district with retail, restaurants, a 180-room hotel, and office space.
DescriptionLong: A massive 71-acre mixed-use walkable district with over 220,000 square feet of retail, more than 100,000 square feet of restaurant space, a 180-room hotel, 90,000 square feet of office space.

Title: NoLi
Description: 150K+ sf adaptive-reuse retail/office/lifestyle district at Lincoln Road and Meridian Ave — Comras Company is reimagining a five-building Miami Beach assemblage into a pedestrian-first 'street off the street' micro-district anchored by Via NoLi and NoLi Plaza.

Title: LILLI
Description: A 53-story boutique waterfront condominium taking shape on Biscayne Bay, bringing just 117 private residences and a wellness-driven amenity program to Miami's Edgewater neighborhood.
DescriptionLong: A new 53-story boutique waterfront condominium from OKO Group rising directly on Biscayne Bay in Miami's Edgewater. Designed by Adrian Smith + Gordon Gill Architecture — the firm behind the Burj Khalifa and Miami's UNA Residences — the project will bring just 117 private one- to four-bedroom residences and penthouses, each with private elevator entry and expansive waterfront views. Sales are officially underway, with residences starting at $1.65 million.
